sections.title: Market Pulse: Tech Sell-off and Geopolitical Tensions Weigh on Sentiment

items.headline: Global Tech Sell-off and U.S.-Iran Tensions Drive Cautious Market Sentiment
items.summary: Market sentiment on July 17, 2026, reflects a complex interplay of easing inflation pressures and strong corporate earnings, significantly tempered by a deep global sell-off in AI chip stocks and ongoing geopolitical tensions between the U.S. and Iran. U.S. stock futures were broadly lower across major indices, indicating a negative opening for Friday's session. While the industrial sector shows robust optimism, the broader market remains under pressure due to concerns over AI valuations and elevated energy risks.

items.statContext: Lower in premarket trading; SPY at $743.70. The S&P 500 fell 0.5% on Thursday.
Significantly lower in premarket; QQQ at $691.92. The Nasdaq fell 1.5% on Thursday.
Lower in premarket trading. The Dow Jones Industrial Average dipped 0.2% on Thursday.

items.headline: Netflix Plunges on Weak Guidance
items.summary: Netflix ($NFLX) shares tumbled 9.68% to $67.15 in premarket trading after the streaming giant issued a weaker-than-expected third-quarter revenue forecast and reported slowing subscriber growth.

items.headline: Intuitive Surgical Slumps Post-Earnings
items.summary: Intuitive Surgical ($ISRG) slumped 11.3% to $357.06 as the growth of its da Vinci robotic surgery system in the U.S. moderated amidst changes in Affordable Care Act subsidies, despite beating Q2 earnings estimates.

items.headline: AST SpaceMobile Falls After Offering
items.summary: AST SpaceMobile ($ASTS) fell 17.04% following a convertible notes offering and a sharp reversal from recent analyst enthusiasm.

items.headline: Abbott Laboratories Jumps on Q2 Earnings
items.summary: Abbott Laboratories ($ABT) jumped 10.71% after reporting its second-quarter earnings, which eased concerns regarding medtech and potential acquisitions.

items.headline: J.B. Hunt Services Rises on Strong Q2
items.summary: J.B. Hunt Transport Services ($JBHT) gained 8.01% following stronger-than-expected second-quarter results and a wave of positive price target increases.

items.headline: FedEx Freight Gains on Analyst Coverage
items.summary: FedEx Freight Holding Company ($FDXF) rose 7.50% after fresh analyst coverage highlighted its crucial role in an anticipated earnings recovery for the freight sector.

items.headline: Semiconductor Stocks Lead Broad Tech Sell-off
items.summary: The semiconductor sector experienced a significant sell-off, extending losses from Thursday and dragging down major indices. Concerns over AI valuations and future capital expenditure, even after strong earnings from companies like TSMC, prompted investors to reduce exposure to AI-linked stocks. Micron ($MU) fell 4.39% to $815.73, Advanced Micro Devices ($AMD) fell 4.82% to $476.80, and Nvidia ($NVDA) was down 2.5% pre-bell.

items.headline: U.S.-Iran Tensions Keep Oil Prices Elevated
items.summary: Escalating military tensions between the U.S. and Iran, with continued U.S. strikes and Iranian warnings, kept crude oil prices near one-month highs. Brent crude was up 1.1% to $85.13 per barrel, and WTI crude rose 1.3% to $79.95 per barrel, reflecting concerns about shipping routes and supply risks.

items.headline: Mixed Economic Data: Easing Inflation, Firm Yields
items.summary: Economic data showed mixed signals, with June producer prices falling 0.3% month-on-month, and core prices rising 0.2%, suggesting an easing of factory-level inflation. However, the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield remained firm around 4.53% to 4.56%. The market anticipates the Federal Reserve will likely keep interest rates unchanged at its July meeting.

items.headline: Industrial Sector Optimism Contrasts with Housing Weakness
items.summary: While the industrial sector's sentiment reached a five-year peak, driven by favorable PMI data and strong demand for data centers, the U.S. housing market presented a challenging picture. Homebuilder confidence slipped for the longest stretch below 40, highlighting divergent trends across the economy.
